_comment: Authoritative list of tool names the apes-runtime ships. Server validates POST /api/agents/:name/tasks payloads against this; the UI's tool-picker fetches it via GET /api/tool-catalog. Adding a new tool means: 1) implement in packages/apes/src/lib/agent-tools/, 2) add an entry here.

tools.name: time.now
tools.description: Current UTC timestamp + ISO date. Useful as a sanity check that tool-calling round-trips.

tools.name: http.get
tools.description: GET an HTTPS URL and return the body (capped at 1MB). Headers are agent-controlled within a deny-list of dangerous values.

tools.name: http.post
tools.description: POST JSON to an HTTPS URL and return the response body. Same caps + header policy as http.get.
tools.inputs: { url: string, body: unknown, headers?: Record<string,string> }

tools.name: file.read
tools.description: Read a file from the agent's home directory ($HOME). Path traversal blocked. Capped at 1MB.

tools.name: file.write
tools.description: Write a file in the agent's home directory ($HOME). Path traversal blocked. 1MB max payload.

tools.name: tasks.list
tools.description: List the owner's open ape-tasks (the user's personal task list at tasks.openape.ai), optionally filtered by status.

tools.name: tasks.create
tools.description: Create a new ape-task on the owner's task list.
tools.inputs: { title: string, notes?: string, priority?: 'low'|'med'|'high', due_at?: string }

tools.name: mail.list
tools.description: List recent inbox messages via o365-cli. Only available if the agent host has o365-cli installed and authenticated.

tools.name: mail.search
tools.description: Search inbox via o365-cli with a query string.

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Run a shell command on the agent host via ape-shell. Every command goes through the DDISA grant cycle — auto-approved by a matching YOLO scope or owner push notification. Runs as the agent's macOS user; file/network access is limited to what that user can see. Returns stdout, stderr, exit code.
